key_theorem.statement: For f ∈ Hölder-s([a,b]) ∩ EML-∞, the best N-term EML-3 approximation (using exp, sin, Gaussian atoms) achieves max error O(N^{-s}), the same rate as N-term Fourier/polynomial approximation.
key_theorem.proof_sketch: Upper bound: EML-3 atoms include Fourier atoms sin(kx), cos(kx) = EML-3. Classical Fourier N-term approx achieves O(N^{-s}) for Hölder-s → so does EML-3 (contains Fourier as a subset). Lower bound: Jackson's theorem gives the optimal rate as O(N^{-s}); EML cannot do better because adding EML depth cannot improve the fundamental smoothness constraint.

approximation_targets.name: sin(x)
approximation_targets.eml_reason: EML-3: sin(x) = Im(exp(ix)) requires 3 EML gates

approximation_targets.name: exp(-x²)
approximation_targets.eml_reason: EML-3: exp(-(x·ln(e))²) — Gaussian is EML-3 despite being smooth

approximation_targets.name: ReLU = max(x,0)
approximation_targets.eml_reason: EML-∞: piecewise linear, not analytic; Hölder-1/2 at x=0 for approximation purposes

approximation_targets.name: Step function 1_{x>0}
approximation_targets.eml_reason: EML-∞: discontinuous, no EML-finite approximation converges uniformly

approximation_targets.name: |x|
approximation_targets.eml_reason: EML-∞: |x| = sqrt(x²); not analytic at 0; rate O(N^{-1})

approximation_targets.name: x·sin(1/x) for x≠0
approximation_targets.eml_reason: EML-∞: oscillates infinitely near 0; Hölder-1/2 regularity

approximation_targets.name: Cantor function (devil's staircase)
approximation_targets.eml_reason: EML-∞: non-differentiable everywhere yet continuous; derivative 0 a.e.

overshoot_N50.eml_interpretation: Step function is EML-∞; any EML-3 (Fourier) approximation cannot converge uniformly. The Gibbs overshoot persists for all N → EML-∞ barrier at the discontinuity.

overshoot_N200.eml_interpretation: Step function is EML-∞; any EML-3 (Fourier) approximation cannot converge uniformly. The Gibbs overshoot persists for all N → EML-∞ barrier at the discontinuity.

convergence_away.comment: Convergence is pointwise O(1/N) away from jump; uniform convergence fails.

shannon_sampling.reason: Band-limited signal = Σ sinc (EML-3) atoms; perfect reconstruction in EML-3

EML_inf_requires_infinite_atoms.why: Non-analytic at 0; EML-3 Fourier requires ∞ terms

eml_depth_summary.Exact representation: EML-k functions are exactly EML-k (no approximation needed)
eml_depth_summary.EML-3 approximation: Dense in C[a,b] by Weierstrass theorem (Session 40)
eml_depth_summary.Gibbs barrier: Discontinuous EML-∞ functions: Gibbs oscillation never disappears
eml_depth_summary.Lower bound: Cannot exceed Hölder smoothness rate regardless of EML depth of atoms

connections.to_session_40: EML Weierstrass theorem: EML-3 atoms are dense in C[a,b]
connections.to_session_37: EML-Fourier: Fourier atoms are EML-3; same approximation theory applies
connections.to_session_69: Chaitin Ω and random sequences are EML-∞; their digit functions cannot be O(N^{-s})-approximated for any s > 0
connections.to_deep_learning: Neural networks with ReLU activations (EML-∞) vs EML-3 activations (smooth): same approximation theory limits
